results.title: Machine Learning Software Engineer (AI) - Fiserv, Inc. -  | Dice.com
highlights:
Machine Learning Software Engineer (AI) - Fiserv, Inc. - | Dice.com
[...]
# Machine Learning Software Engineer (AI)
[...]
Machine Learning (ML)
[...]
Fiserv
[...]
Calling all innovators - find your future at Fiserv. We're Fiserv, a global leader in Fintech and payments, and we move money and information in a way that moves the world. We connect financial institutions, corporations, merchants, and consumers to one another millions of times a day - quickly, reliably, and securely. Any time you swipe your credit card, pay through a mobile app, or withdraw money from the bank, we're involved. If you want to make an impact on a global scale, come make a difference at Fiserv. Job Title Machine Learning Software Engineer (AI) What does a successful Machine Learning Software Engineer (AI) do at Fiserv? At Fiserv, you build production machine learning software that powers real-time decisioning at scale across payments and financial services. You translate model concepts into reliable services and data pipelines with strong performance, observability, and operational discipline. You partner with product, data science, and platform engineering to deliver measurable outcomes and continuously improve models in production. What you will do
[...]
- Architect, design, and implement scalable ML models and real-time inference services for high-throughput, low-latency decisioning.
[...]
- Build and optimize data ingestion and feature pipelines using Kafka, Airflow, and Spark to enable consistent, high-quality feature extraction.
- Collaborate with data scientists and product engineers to translate complex business requirements into technical specifications and deployable ML solutions.
- Conduct structured experimentation using frameworks such as TensorFlow and PyTorch, selecting approaches that balance accuracy, explainability, and production constraints.
- Deploy and operate ML applications using Docker and Kubernetes in cloud environments, including release automation, monitoring, and ongoing optimization.
[...]
What you must have
[...]
- 7+ years of experience in machine learning and data science.
- 7+ years of experience deploying AI solutions in cloud environments.
- 5+ years of experience driving large-scale projects in a matrixed environment (project management and/or consulting).
- Bachelor's degree in computer science, data science, or a related field.
[...]
What would be nice to have
[...]
- 6+ years of experience in the financial services industry.
- 5+ years of proficiency in Python, Java, or Scala, including hands-on use of ML libraries (TensorFlow, Keras, Scikit-learn).
- 4+ years of experience with SQL and NoSQL databases (PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Cassandra).
[...]
Employers have access to artificial intelligence language tools (“AI”) that help generate and enhance job descriptions and AI may have been used to create this description. The position description has been reviewed for accuracy and Dice believes it to correctly reflect the job opportunity.
[...]
- Dice Id: 90922487
- Position Id: 24376433
- Posted 2 days ago

results.title: AI Software Engineer – Fellow I at Fiserv | Remote Job | Kickstart Remote
highlights:
AI Software Engineer – Fellow I at Fiserv | Remote Job | Kickstart Remote
[...]
# AI Software Engineer – Fellow I
[...]
### Job Description
[...]
Engineering lead building a multi-tenant agentic AI platform for financial services with a focus on security and technical standards. Leading a high-performing team to drive platform success.
[...]
#### Responsibilities:
[...]
- Own end-to-end engineering delivery of the agentic AI platform
[...]
- Set technical vision and architecture direction for a multi-tenant agentic platform built on AWS Bedrock AgentCore, Strands Agents SDK, and Cedar/AVP authorization
[...]
- Recruit, hire, and develop pillar leads and senior engineers across Identity & Access, Agent Runtime, Observability & FinOps, Catalog & Registry, and Evals & Testing
[...]
- Drive platform security posture — threat modeling for agent delegation chains, prompt injection defence, cross-tenant isolation, and regulatory compliance (SOC2, OCC, FFIEC)
[...]
- Establish engineering standards — ADRs, SDK architecture patterns, code review culture
[...]
- Build and operate the agent onboarding pipeline — enabling adjacent business teams to build, certify, and deploy agents on the platform
[...]
- Define SLOs, operational readiness criteria, and incident response processes for a banking-grade production platform
[...]
- Manage cross-pillar dependencies and technical risk — credential flows, MCP Gateway governance, audit pipeline completeness, and multi-tenant data isolation
[...]
- Partner with Security to establish pre-GA sign-off gates including penetration testing, SAST/DAST integration, and compliance certification
[...]
- Drive FinOps discipline — per-tenant cost attribution, LLM usage optimization, and infrastructure right-sizing
[...]
#### Requirements:
[...]
- 10+ years of software engineering experience
- 5+ years leading engineering teams across multiple product areas or platform pillars
- Proven track record building and shipping multi-tenant SaaS platforms — ideally in financial services, payments, or regulated industries
[...]
- Deep hands-on experience with AWS (IAM, KMS, Cognito, DynamoDB, S3, Lambda, Step Functions, ECS/EKS) and infrastructure-as-code (CDK or Terraform)
[...]
- Strong background in platform engineering — SDK design, API governance, developer experience, and internal platform adoption
[...]
- Experience with identity and authorization architectures — federation, RBAC/ABAC, credential management, tenant isolation patterns
[...]
- Demonstrated ability to recruit, develop, and retain senior engineering talent in a high-growth environment
[...]
- Experience managing AWS or cloud vendor partnerships with embedded engineering resources
[...]
- Familiarity with security and compliance in financial services — threat modeling, penetration testing, audit requirements, data residency
[...]
- Strong architectural judgment — ability to make build-vs-buy decisions, set technical direction across distributed teams, and manage technical debt
[...]
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field (or equivalent experience).
[...]
Staff Software Engineer working with the founding team to shape the future of AI-powered telemetry solutions. Collaborating with product engineering teams to develop and integrate new AI features.

results.title: AI Transformation Consultant - Fiserv, Inc. -  | Dice.com
highlights:
AI Transformation Consultant - Fiserv, Inc. - | Dice.com
[...]
# AI Transformation Consultant
[...]
Machine Learning (ML)
[...]
Calling all innovators - find your future at Fiserv. We're Fiserv, a global leader in Fintech and payments, and we move money and information in a way that moves the world. We connect financial institutions, corporations, merchants, and consumers to one another millions of times a day - quickly, reliably, and securely. Any time you swipe your credit card, pay through a mobile app, or withdraw money from the bank, we're involved. If you want to make an impact on a global scale, come make a difference at Fiserv. Job Title AI Transformation Consultant About your role: As a AI Transformation Consultant you will lead the end to end design, implementation, and adoption of artificial intelligence solutions that drive measurable business outcomes. This role partners closely with business leaders, product teams, and technical experts to identify high value AI use cases, translate business needs into scalable AI capabilities, and ensure successful deployment across the organization. You will serve as a critical bridge between strategy and execution-enabling change adoption, governance, and ethical use of AI while ensuring alignment with enterprise systems, regulatory requirements, and performance objectives. In addition you will analyze complex data sets, assess business problems, and derive actionable, data-driven insights What you will do: AI Strategy & Use Case Development -Partner with clients and internal stakeholders to understand business objectives and identify high impact AI use cases. -Define AI roadmaps and transformation strategies aligned to organizational priorities and long term value creation. AI Solution Design & Implementation -Lead the design, development, and deployment of AI and machine learning solutions across business and technology platforms -Ensure seamless integration of AI solutions with existing systems, data pipelines, and operational processes. Data Analysis & Model Development -Assess data readiness, quality, and governance to support AI initiatives. -Design and guide the development of AI/ML models tailored to specific business challenges and operational workflows. Stakeholder & Change Management -Act as a trusted advisor and liaison between technical teams and non technical stakeholders. -Translate complex AI concepts into clear, actionable insights and business recommendations for executive and operational audiences. Training, Adoption & Enablement -Support organizational readiness by delivering training, best practices, and enablement sessions on AI tools and capabilities. -Drive adoption by embedding AI solutions into day to day workflows and decision making processes. Performance, Governance & Ethics -Monitor AI system performance, accuracy, and business impact post implementation. -Ensure AI solutions comply with ethical standards, regulatory requirements, data privacy policies, and internal governance frameworks. Experience you will need to have:
[...]
- 10+ years of experience in artificial intelligence, machine learning, data analytics, software development, or related technical disciplines.
- 8+ years of experience in machine learning, natural language processing, data analytics, and AI platforms.
- 8+ years of experience working with structured and unstructured data, model lifecycle management, and enterprise data ecosystems.
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, or a related field; or equivalent work experience.
[...]
- This role is on-site Monday through Friday. Fiserv considers in-person collaboration to be an essential part of this role as in-person office experiences help you with your overall onboarding experience and leads to stronger productivity.
[...]
is a full-time, direct-hire position, and no contract options for unsolicited agency submissions will be considered.

# Technology Analyst Program – Data Engineer (Berkeley Heights, NJ)

On Site Full Time New Jersey, United States Fiserv

Fiserv is a global leader in Fintech and payments, and we move money and information in a way that moves the world. We’re looking for an early-career Data Engineer to help design, build, and support reliable data pipelines that move data from source systems into analytics and operational platforms.

## Requirements

- Post a Job
- Recent graduate of a bachelor’s degree completed in December 2024 or after in Computer Science, Data Science, Management Information Systems, Technical Project Management, or other Technology Degrees
- 3.0+ GPA
- 0-2 years of professional work experience

## Benefits

- Formal training
- Mentorship
- Exposure to senior leadership

To apply for this job please visit fiserv.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com.

[...]
We're Fiserv

**Job Title**

Technology Analyst Program - Data Engineer (Berkeley Heights, NJ)
[...]
**2026 Technology Analyst Program**
[...]
The Fiserv Technology Analyst Program is a two-year, early career development experience designed to accelerate your growth in the fintech industry. Through two structured assignments within a specific technology track, analysts gain hands-on experience, build technical and professional skills, and collaborate across teams to solve real business challenges. As a performance-driven company, Fiserv is committed to developing emerging talent and creating opportunities for those who demonstrate initiative, growth, and impact. The program includes formal training, mentorship, and exposure to senior leadership, supporting long-term career development within the organization.
[...]
**What does a successful Technology Analyst- Data Engineer do?**
[...]
We’re looking for an early-career Data Engineer to help design, build, and support reliable data pipelines that move data from source systems into analytics and operational platforms. You’ll work closely with data engineers, analysts, and stakeholders to ensure data is accurate, timely, and scalable.
[...]
**What you will do:**
[...]
* Assist in designing and building data pipelines for data ingestion, transformation, and storage.
* Develop and maintain pipeline workflows to enable efficient data movement and processing.
* Support data modeling and schema design for analytics and operational use cases.
* Monitor, troubleshoot, and improve pipeline performance, reliability, and data quality.
[...]
**What you will need to have:**
[...]
* Recent graduate of a bachelor's degree completed in December 2024 or after in Computer Science, Data Science, Management Information Systems, Technical Project Management, or other Technology Degrees 
* 3.0+ GPA 
* 0-2 years of professional work experience 
**What would be great to have:**
[...]
* Proficiency in SQL and a solid understanding of relational databases.
* Basic knowledge of Python or Java (or similar scripting languages) for automation and data processing.
* Understanding of data warehousing concepts and familiarity with cloud platforms (Azure or similar).
* Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work with large datasets.
* Experience with Apache Spark, Hadoop, or similar big data frameworks.
* Familiarity with Databricks workflows or other workflow orchestration tools.
* Exposure to Databricks, Azure Data Factory, or GCP Dataflow.
* Knowledge of CI/CD practices and pipelines for data engineering.

highlights:
Developer Relations Technical Solutions Engineer | Hirequorum

Hirequorum.← All Jobs

# Developer Relations Technical Solutions Engineer

Posted Jun 6, 2026

Calling all innovators – find your future at Fiserv.

We’re Fiserv, a global leader in Fintech and payments, and we move money and information in a way that moves the world. We connect financial institutions, corporations, merchants, and consumers to one another millions of times a day – quickly, reliably, and securely. Any time you swipe your credit card, pay through a mobile app, or withdraw money from the bank, we’re involved. If you want to make an impact on a global scale, come make a difference at Fiserv.

Job Title

Developer Relations Technical Solutions Engineer

Developer Relations Technical Solutions EngineerThank you for considering employment with Fiserv. Please:

- Apply using your legal name
- Complete the step-by-step profile and attach your resume (either is acceptable, both are preferable).

### Our commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:

Fiserv is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, protected veteran status, or any other category protected by law.

Note to agencies:

Fiserv does not accept resume submissions from agencies outside of existing agreements. Please do not send resumes to Fiserv associates. Fiserv is not responsible for any fees associated with unsolicited resume submissions.

Warning about fake job posts:

Please be aware of fraudulent job postings that are not affiliated with Fiserv. Fraudulent job postings may be used by cyber criminals to target your personally identifiable information and/or to steal money or financial information. Any communications from a Fiserv representative will come from a legitimate Fiserv email address.

Apply for This Job

results.title: Fiserv Internship 2026: Technology Summer Intern Chennai
highlights:
# Fiserv Internship 2026: Technology Summer Intern in Chennai – Apply Now
[...]
Fiserv, a Fortune 500 global fintech leader powering billions of transactions annually, is inviting applications for its 2026 Technology Summer Intern program in Chennai. This 10-week paid internship offers engineering students hands-on exposure to enterprise-grade systems in application development, cybersecurity, mobile solutions, network engineering, and quality assurance. Interns work alongside senior engineers on live product backlogs, receive structured training and mentorship, and participate in leadership sessions and site tours. For freshers from 2024/2025/2026 batches, this is a strong launchpad into the fintech domain, with potential for a pre-placement offer (PPO) based on performance.
[...]
| Company | Fiserv |
| --- | --- |
| Role | Technology – Summer Intern |
| Location | Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India |
| Experience | Freshers / Intern |
| Education | Bachelors degree in Computer Science, IT, ECE, Engineering, Mathematics, MIS, or any STEM stream |
| Salary | ₹3.5 – 5.5 LPA (estimated) |
[...]
To apply, you must be pursuing a Bachelors degree in CS, IT, ECE, Engineering, Mathematics, MIS, or any STEM stream from the 2024, 2025, or 2026 batch. A minimum GPA of 3.0 on a 4.0 scale (roughly 60% or 6.5 CGPA) is required; a 3.5 GPA is preferred. While any STEM background is accepted, candidates with coursework in programming, databases, or data analytics have an edge. You must have unrestricted work authorization and not require future sponsorship. The role is in-office in Chennai, so proximity or willingness to relocate is essential.
[...]
- Support design, coding, testing, and documentation of business applications under senior engineering mentorship.
[...]
- Participate in test planning, automation script execution, and defect tracking for high-volume payment systems.
[...]
- Contribute to user-facing mobile and web modules that handle real financial transactions.
[...]
- Assist in vulnerability scanning, secure code reviews, and basic threat-modelling tasks.
[...]
- Write SQL queries, validate data pipelines, and help create dashboards using PowerBI or similar tools.
[...]
- Capture process flows, user guides, and runbooks for systems worked on.
[...]
- Join Agile/Scrum ceremonies including daily stand-ups, sprint planning, and retrospectives.
[...]
The Fiserv Internship 2026 selection process follows a standard workflow: 1) Online Application Review – resumes and academic records are screened against eligibility; 2) Online Assessment – aptitude and coding/technical assessment covering MCQs, logic, basic programming, and core CS fundamentals; 3) Technical Interview – a 30-45 minute interview on DSA, OOPS, DBMS, projects, and problem-solving; 4) HR Discussion – cultural fit, internship goals, availability for the 10-week program, and offer confirmation. The entire process typically takes 2-4 weeks, with assessments scheduled in rolling batches.

results.title: Application Specialist - Analyst Program | Wfh
highlights: the bank, we’re involved. If you want to make an impact on a global scale, come make a difference at Fiserv. Job Title Application Specialist - Analyst Program 2026 Technology Analyst Program The Fiserv Technology Analyst Program is a two-year, early career development experience designed to accelerate your growth in the fintech industry. Through two structured assignments within a specific technology track, analysts gain hands-on experience, build technical and professional skills, and collaborate across teams to solve real business challenges. As a performance-driven company, Fiserv is committed to developing emerging talent and creating opportunities for those who demonstrate initiative, growth, and impact. The program includes formal training, mentorship, and exposure to senior leadership, supporting long-term career development within the organization. Job Title: Application Specialist - Analyst Program What does a successful Application Specialist - Analyst Program do? As the Application Specialist, you will play a meaningful role supporting Fiserv’s Deposit Solutions environment, including Enterprise SCO, Fraud and/or TCM systems. You will work closely with internal channel partners and external clients to troubleshoot technical issues, advise on integration and configuration, and guide clients on regulatory and processing questions. A successful associate applies strong case-management principles, high organization, and a service-oriented mindset to deliver best‑in‑class support for our banking clients. What you will do: Serve as the principal point of contact for external and internal customers regarding training, application queries and issues. Research and diagnose problems to determine resolutions for reported incidents. Escalate complex issues efficiently to Tier 3 support and track remediation through closure. Participate in User Acceptance Testing (UAT) and review product documentation for accuracy and client impact. Provide client input to Product Managers, Business Analysts and Developers regarding product features and enhancements. Analyze incidents and trends to identify opportunities to improve service quality and client satisfaction. Develop and share knowledge articles, runbooks and client-facing documentation to enable team and client success. Support scheduled team hours of 8:00 AM–5:00 PM onsite in Milwaukee, WI and assist with rotating extended support coverage as needed. What you will need to have: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Management Information Systems, Cybersecurity, Technical Project Management, or other technology degree completed December 2024 or after; 3.0+ GPA and/or equivalent military experience. 0-2 years of professional work experience. Ability to work onsite in Milwaukee, WI (regular business hours with occasional rotation for extended coverage). What would be great to have: 3+ years of experience with check/image processing or fraud systems (for example, X9, Check 21, ImageCash). 3+ years of experience supporting enterprise deposit systems (for example, Enterprise SCO, TCM). 3+ years of experience using ticketing systems such as ServiceNow or JIRA for incident and case management. 3+ years of experience scripting or automating tasks using Python or PowerShell. Perks at Work: • We’re #FiservProud of our commitment to your overall well‑being with a growing offering of physical, mental, emotional, and financial benefits from day one. • Maintain a healthy work‑life balance with paid holidays, generous time off policies, including Recharge & Refuel time for qualifying associates, and free counseling through our EAP. • Plan for your future with competitive salaries, the Fiserv 401(k) Savings Plan, and our Employee Stock Purchase Plan. • Recognize and be recognized by colleagues with our Living Proof program where you can exchange points for a variety of rewards. • Prioritize your health with a variety of medical, dental, vision, life and

Fiserv Brings in Cognition AI ...

# Fiserv Brings in Cognition AI Agent to Automate Software Engineering
[...]
Fiserv has deployed Cognition's AI agent software engineer to automate development tasks. The Silicon Review reports on the fintech giant's move to boost productivity as AI coding tools reshape software development.
[...]
Fiserv has brought in Cognition's AI agent software engineer to automate software development tasks across its engineering organization. The fintech giant is deploying the artificial intelligence tool to write, debug, and maintain code as financial services companies accelerate their adoption of AI coding assistants.
[...]
The Cognition AI agent is designed to handle complex engineering tasks autonomously, reducing the time developers spend on routine coding and allowing them to focus on higher-value architecture and product work. The tool integrates into existing development workflows and can be assigned specific engineering tasks through natural language prompts.
[...]
Fiserv's move to deploy the Cognition AI agent reflects a broader trend in the financial services industry, where banks and payment processors are adopting AI coding tools to accelerate digital transformation. The fintech sector has been particularly aggressive in adopting AI to modernize legacy systems and launch new products faster.
[...]
Cognition, a San Francisco-based startup founded in 2024, has raised significant funding from investors including Peter Thiel's Founders Fund. The company's AI agent software engineer has been adopted by several Fortune 500 companies across technology, finance, and healthcare sectors.
[...]
The deployment comes as AI coding tools face scrutiny over code quality, security, and intellectual property concerns. Fiserv has implemented guardrails around the Cognition AI agent's use, including code review requirements, access controls, and monitoring to ensure compliance with financial industry regulations.
[...]
As Fiserv brings in Cognition's AI agent software engineer, The Silicon Review examines how financial services companies are adopting autonomous coding tools to boost developer productivity and accelerate software delivery.
[...]
Q: What is the Cognition AI agent?A: The Cognition AI agent is an artificial intelligence tool that writes, debugs, and maintains code autonomously. It can be assigned specific engineering tasks through natural language prompts.
[...]
Q: Why is Fiserv deploying the Cognition AI agent?A: Fiserv is deploying the Cognition AI agent to automate routine coding tasks, reduce development time, and allow engineers to focus on higher-value architecture and product work.
[...]
Q: How does the Cognition AI agent integrate into development workflows?A: The Cognition AI agent integrates into existing development workflows and can handle complex engineering tasks autonomously, including writing code, debugging, and maintaining existing codebases.
[...]
Q: What industries are adopting AI coding agents like Cognition?A: Technology, finance, and healthcare sectors are adopting AI coding agents. The fintech sector has been particularly aggressive in adopting AI to modernize legacy systems.
[...]
Q: What concerns exist about AI coding tools?A: AI coding tools face scrutiny over code quality, security vulnerabilities, and intellectual property concerns. Fiserv has implemented guardrails including code review requirements and access controls.
[...]
Q: Who are Cognition's investors?A: Cognition has raised funding from investors including Peter Thiel's Founders Fund and other venture capital firms.

results.title: Fiserv Launches agentOS: The Operating System for Agentic AI in Banking
highlights:
# Fiserv Launches agentOS: The Operating System for Agentic AI in Banking
[...]
Designed to help financial institutions safely deploy and scale AI agents
[...]
NEW YORK, May 14, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Fiserv, Inc.(NASDAQ: FISV) a leading global provider of payments and financial technology solutions, today launched agentOS, an agentic AI operating system designed to help financial institutions deploy, manage, and scale AI agents across their banking workflows. Six financial institutions have partnered with Fiserv to co-develop agentOS, with two running agents in beta today. agentOS is expected to be widely available by August 2026.
[...]
agentOS features the industry’s first agent marketplace built natively for banking workflows allowing financial institutions to access Fiserv-built agents, build their own, or deploy third-party agents within a controlled architecture. The marketplace will initially feature four Fiserv-built agents and nine third-party agents supporting tasks spanning risk management, regulatory reporting, deposit operations, and back-office reconciliation to bring agentic automation to the full breadth of banking workflows.
[...]
agentOS is supported by strategic collaborations with OpenAI and Amazon Web Services (AWS) to bring frontier AI to financial institutions at scale.
[...]
“AI will fundamentally reshape how financial institutions operate,” said Dhivya Suryadevara, Co-President of Fiserv. “agentOS is
[...]
first place where banks can run Fiserv'
[...]
agents, build their own, and deploy from a curated set of partners — all under the same governance, identity,
[...]
audit controls. The pilots are already proving it works —
[...]
-developing the
[...]
Developed using AI-first engineering principles, agentOS combines enterprise-grade reliability with governance by design, including identity-bound execution, policy enforcement, observability and traceability. The platform allows financial institutions to deploy AI agents confidently across service, fraud, payments, compliance and growth workflows.
[...]
agentOS will initially feature four Fiserv agents: Commercial Loan Onboarding, Daily Operational Analysis and Reporting, Agentic Deposit Intelligence, and Agentic AML Triage Analysis. Fiserv will expand its portfolio of first-party agents in the future, with a focus on other time-consuming workflows—including fraud, servicing, compliance, risk management, deposit operations, and growth.
[...]
Committed to fostering open ecosystems, Fiserv is also partnering on some of the most compelling agentic use cases for banks. Currently, nine third-party partners have joined through agentOS Marketplace, supporting use cases ranging from customer engagement and financial crimes compliance to deposit intelligence, regulatory compliance, dispute management, and reconciliation.
[...]
Strategic Collaborators: OpenAI and Amazon Web Services
[...]
Fiserv is developing select first-party agents with OpenAI and partnering strategically to bring frontier reasoning into the workflows that move money across core, payments, issuer processing, and servicing.
[...]
agentOS leverages Amazon Bedrock AgentCore to provide secure access to leading AI models, giving financial institutions flexibility as the technology evolves.
[...]
of AWS to
[...]
look forward to
[...]
First Interstate Bank and Boulder Dam Credit Union are running pilots on agentOS today, with measurable results across commercial loan onboarding and operational reporting. Salem Five, City National Bank, Bank OZK, and SouthState are co-developing the next wave of agents with Fiserv, with deployments beginning this summer. Close collaboration with these institutions has been central to building an operating system that works for banks.
[...]
With agentOS, Fiserv brings the scale, expertise, and trust financial institutions require to deploy agentic AI within a governed, audit-ready architecture and convert it into durable operational progress.

results.title: Security Analyst: Governance, Risk and Compliance - Analyst Program I | Wfh
highlights: the bank, we're involved. If you want to make an impact on a global scale, come make a difference at Fiserv. Job Title Security Analyst: Governance, Risk and Compliance - Analyst Program I Security Analyst: Governance, Risk and Compliance What does a successful Security Analyst-Governance, Risk and Compliance do? We are looking for a proactive GRC analyst to join our Technology Analyst Program. In this role, you will analyze application security controls, perform vendor risk assessments, and assist in testing internal frameworks (like SOC 2 or NIST). You will be a key player in ensuring our new and existing business applications remain compliant and resilient for our clients. What you will do: You’ll assist in evaluating third-party vendor assessments and documenting internal risks. Audit Support: Gathering evidence for audits like PCI or SOC 2. This might involve taking screenshots of system settings or pulling logs to prove that only authorized people have access to data. Policy Management: Reviewing and updating security policies example, updating the Remote Work Policy to include guidelines for using generative AI. Compliance Monitoring: Using GRC tools (like Archer, or ServiceNow) to track security rules are followed. What would be great to have: Technical Foundation: General understanding of Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC) Android Development: Foundational experience in Android development (Android Studio, SDK components, and mobile UI basics). Technical Skills: Frameworks: Familiarity with PCI, NIST CSF, or SOC 2. Cloud Security: Basic understanding of GCP Cloud security. Data Privacy: General knowledge of GDPR or CCPA. AI Governance: Understanding the risks of LLMs (data leakage, prompt injection). Soft Skills: Attention to Detail: You are the "quality control" for security. Communication: Can you explain a technical risk to engineering or legal teams. Project Management: GRC involves tracking multiple small tasks simultaneously. What you will need to have: Recent graduate of a bachelor's degree completed in December 2024 or after in Computer Science, Data Science, Management Information Systems, Cybersecurity, Technical Project Management, or other Technology Degrees 3.0+ GPA 0-2 years of professional work experience Important info about this role: This role is on-site Monday through Friday. Fiserv considers in-person collaboration to be an essential part of this role as in-person office experiences help you with your overall onboarding experience and leads to stronger productivity. You must currently possess valid and unrestricted U.S. work authorization to be considered for this role. Individuals with temporary visas including, but not limited to, F-1 (OPT, CPT, STEM), H-1B, H-2, or TN, or any candidate requiring sponsorship, now or in the future, will not be considered for this role. This is a full-time, direct-hire position, and no contract options for unsolicited agency submissions will be considered. All offers of employment are contingent on standard background checks. Fiserv and certain of its affiliated companies are federal, state, and/or local government contractors. Should this position support a Federal Government contract, now or in the future, the successful candidate will be subject to a background check conducted by the U.S. Government to determine eligibility and suitability for federal contract employment for public trust or sensitive positions. Positions that support state and/or local contracts also may require additional background checks to determine eligibility and suitability. Application Ends: 3/20/26 Salary Range $70,800.00 - $106,200.00 These pay ranges apply to employees in Sunnyvale, California. Pay ranges for employees in other states may differ. It is unlawful to discriminate against a prospective employee due to the individual's status as a veteran. This position is not eligible for an annual incentive opportunity and is also not eligible to earn commissions. Thank you for

results.title: Fiserv Bets On AI Engineer Devin To Speed Core Banking Modernization - Simply Wall St News
highlights:
- Fiserv (NasdaqGS:FISV) announced a partnership with Cognition to deploy its AI software engineer Devin across Fiserv platform engineering.
- The initiative focuses on accelerating core banking modernization and large scale systems development across the Fiserv ecosystem.
- The move positions Fiserv among the early large fintech adopters of autonomous AI tools for product development and release management.
[...]
By using Devin for software engineering tasks, Fiserv is aiming to shorten development cycles, cut manual workload, and support more frequent product updates for clients. For you, the main questions will be how quickly this shows up in product delivery, client satisfaction, and the company’s competitive positioning versus other core banking and payments providers that are also investing in AI tools.
[...]
This partnership is part of Fiserv’s push to use AI to speed up how it builds and upgrades its core banking platforms, which are competing with offerings from companies such as FIS and Global Payments. Deploying Devin across core-platform modernization and other engineering projects is aimed at compressing long release cycles in an area that is usually slow and resource intensive. If Devin can reliably handle tasks like reading large codebases, writing and testing code, and running in parallel with human teams, Fiserv could expand engineering capacity without a matching increase in headcount. The company is also tying this to tighter governance and security around AI-assisted development, which matters given regulatory scrutiny on financial software quality and resilience. For investors, the key angle is whether this AI-first approach helps Fiserv ship stable features faster than peers while avoiding setbacks from software defects, security issues, or integration problems.
[...]
- The move directly supports the narrative focus on modernization and AI-powered platforms by using Devin to speed up work on core systems that underpin recurring software and services revenue.
- It also touches on execution risk raised in the narrative, because success depends on Fiserv integrating autonomous AI into complex banking codebases without disrupting reliability for financial institutions.
- Large-scale use of an autonomous AI engineer on internal platforms is not deeply covered in the narrative, which concentrates more on client-facing products like Clover and CashFlow Central than on how development is done behind the scenes.
[...]
- ⚠️ Large-scale AI-assisted coding on critical banking infrastructure introduces operational and cyber risk if governance or testing falls short, which could affect client trust and regulatory scrutiny.
- ⚠️ If Devin does not integrate smoothly with existing engineering workflows, Fiserv could face disruption costs and slower delivery at a time when peers such as FIS and Global Payments are also investing in AI tools.
- 🎁 If Devin reliably shortens release cycles while maintaining quality, Fiserv could bring new banking capabilities to clients more quickly, supporting its aim of being a preferred partner for core modernization projects.
- 🎁 Using AI to extend engineering capacity may allow Fiserv to handle more modernization work without equivalent cost growth, which could support margin resilience if executed carefully.
[...]
From here, it is worth tracking how often Fiserv links Devin to specific product launches, modernization wins, or improvements in platform stability in its updates. Any commentary on implementation times for banks, broader use of AI in testing and quality checks, or changes in development spending could give clues about whether the partnership is delivering practical benefits. Investors can also watch how regulators and major financial institution clients respond to AI-assisted development on core systems, and whether competitors adopt similar tools or choose more cautious approaches.

Fiserv Inc. (NASDAQ: FISV) continues to position itself as a leader in banking modernization through the strategic deployment of artificial intelligence (AI). Executive leadership emphasizes a three‑pillar strategy of stabilisation, attachment, and growth across the firm’s core divisions, underscoring significant capital allocation toward service resilience and technology innovation.

results.title: Credit Risk Analyst | Jobflarely
highlights: innovators – find your future at Fiserv. We’re Fiserv, a global leader in Fintech and payments, and we move money and information in a way that moves the world. We connect financial institutions, corporations, merchants, and consumers to one another millions of times a day – quickly, reliably, and securely. Any time you swipe your credit card, pay through a mobile app, or withdraw money from the bank, we’re involved. If you want to make an impact on a global scale, come make a difference at Fiserv. Job Title Credit Risk Analyst What does a successful Risk Analyst do at Fiserv? Under the guidance of a Director or Portfolio Manager, you will support transaction risk management for a portfolio of merchant accounts. What you will do: Develop, test, and implement machine learning models and rules to mitigate fraud and credit risk. Analyze ambiguous data to understand trends, reach conclusions, and develop strategies to improve portfolio performance. Provide internal and external reporting and analytics on an ongoing and ad hoc basis. Monitor key metrics and ensures accounts are meeting all contractual SLAs. Communicate directly with external customers. Work with minimal supervision. What you will need to have: Bachelor's degree 1 year of experience in an analytical role or educational equivalent. Strong background in a technical or analytical environment. Experience working with, understanding, and analyzing large data sets. Experience with SQL What would be great to have: Masters' degree Python, R Studio, or other statistical languages. Experience with SAS. Tableau, Power BI, and other data analytics and visualization tools. Predictive modeling and big data experience. Background in a modeling and testing (champion/challenger) environment. An understanding of the check/ACH/payments industry. #LI-JS1 This role is not eligible to be performed in Colorado, California, District of Columbia, Hawaii, Illinois, Maryland, Minnesota, New Jersey, New York, Nevada, Rhode Island or Washington. It is unlawful to discriminate against a prospective employee due to the individual's status as a veteran. Please note that salary ranges provided for this role on external job boards are salary estimates made by outside parties and may not be accurate. Thank you for considering employment with Fiserv. Please: Apply using your legal name Complete the step-by-step profile and attach your resume (either is acceptable, both are preferable). Our commitment to Equal Opportunity: Fiserv is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, protected veteran status, or any other category protected by law. If you have a disability and require a reasonable accommodation in completing a job application or otherwise participating in the overall hiring process, please contact . Please note our AskHR representatives do not have visibility to your application status. Current associates who require a workplace accommodation should refer to Fiserv’s Disability Accommodation Policy for additional information. Note to agencies: Fiserv does not accept resume submissions from agencies outside of existing agreements. Please do not send resumes to Fiserv associates. Fiserv is not responsible for any fees associated with unsolicited resume submissions. Warning about fake job posts: Please be aware of fraudulent job postings that are not affiliated with Fiserv. Fraudulent job postings may be used by cyber criminals to target your personally identifiable information and/or to steal money or financial information. Any communications from a Fiserv representative will come from a legitimate Fiserv email address. There's a reason why Fiserv is the global leader in information management and electronic commerce systems for the financial services industry. It's our people. If you are a talented

results.title: Fiserv has co-created AI agents with six banks and OpenAI | American Banker
highlights:
Fiserv has co-created AI agents with six banks and OpenAI | American Banker
[...]
# Fiserv has co-created AI agents with six banks and OpenAI
[...]
- Key insight: The major core banking software providers are co-developing AI agents compatible with their cores; the latest example is a collaboration between Fiserv, OpenAI and AWS.
- What's at stake:
[...]
seek efficiency from these agents. There's still a strong need for these agents to operate within guardrails and compliance rules.
[...]
- Forward look
[...]
are likely to make
[...]
The financial industry's race to adopt agentic AI has put pressure on the largest bank software vendors to support this activity. In response, Fiserv and FIS, providers of foundation technology underpinning most U.S. banks, have begun offering AI agents that work with their software. BMO and Amalgamated Bank are deploying a new Financial Crimes AI agent FIS recently built with Anthropic that helps investigate financial crime and money laundering.
[...]
On Thursday, Fiserv, which has 6,000 financial institution customers (3,000 use its core systems), announced it's launched an agentic AI operating system for AI agents that work with its core and payments software. The operating system, agentOS, was developed with OpenAI using Amazon Web Services' Bedrock. Fiserv is also working with OpenAI on other agentic AI projects.
[...]
Dhivya Suryadevara, who joined Fiserv five months ago as co‑president, said she's talked to dozens of clients about innovation and the problems they're trying to solve.
[...]
AgentOS lets banks use Fiserv-built agents, build their own or deploy third-party agents within a controlled architecture that has "bank-grade controls." These are controls for policy, governance, auditability and proper recordkeeping, Suryadevara said.
[...]
So far, Fiserv has co-developed four AI agents with six bank partners.
[...]
Fiserv is also codeveloping agents with Salem Five, City National Bank, Bank OZK and SouthState; deployments will begin this summer.
[...]
Nine software companies have signed up so far to create AI agents for agentOS: Arva, Cognext, iTuring.ai, Lumio, Osfin.ai, Sardine, Sierra, Tracfox and Trulioo.
[...]
AgentOS runs on AWS Bedrock, a service that provides a single application programming interface to access and use foundation models from AI companies like Anthropic, Meta, Mistral, Cohere and Amazon to build generative AI applications.
[...]
we want to
[...]
they have the
[...]
Fiserv's collaboration with OpenAI is broader. The two companies are building AI agents together. They're working together to use AI to help with software conversions and modernization projects. Software deployments and upgrades can take thousands of hours and force banks to put other projects on hold, Suryadevara pointed out. "With AI, there's a tremendous opportunity to collapse the effort and the timeline associated with implementations and conversions, and we're going to work with OpenAI to say, how do we approach these in an AI-native way," she said.
[...]
In a third collaborative effort, the two companies will infuse AI models into existing bank products and operations. In the fourth element of the partnership, Fiserv is joining OpenAI's Trusted Access for Cyber program.
[...]
"That's going to help us infuse AI into the cyber capabilities that we already offer to our banks," she said. Suryadevara declined to say whether this is an answer to Anthropic's Claude Mythos model, which can detect and potentially exploit software vulnerabilities faster than existing software or humans.
[...]
"Many people are saying that AI is one of the most transformative technologies that are going to impact banking in a positive way, and I think that everybody in the ecosystem should work together to bring the right solutions in our clients' hands," Suryadevara said. "So we're supportive of AI efforts in the industry across the board." Fiserv already works with Google and Anthropic in some places, she said.

results.title: Fiserv Selects OpenAI and AWS to Launch agentOS - Channel Drive
highlights:
# Fiserv Selects OpenAI and AWS to Launch agentOS
[...]
#### Fiserv, Inc. a global provider of payments and financial technology solutions, has launched agentOS, an agentic AI operating system designed to help financial institutions deploy, manage, and scale AI agents across their banking workflows. Six financial institutions have partnered with Fiserv to co-develop agentOS, with two running agents in beta today. agentOS is expected to be widely available by August 2026.
[...]
agentOS features the industry
[...]
agentOS is supported by strategic collaborations with OpenAI and Amazon Web Services (AWS) to bring frontier AI to financial institutions at scale.
[...]
“AI will fundamentally reshape how financial institutions operate,” said Dhivya Suryadevara, Co-President of Fiserv. “agentOS is the first place where banks can run Fiserv’s agents, build their own, and deploy from a curated set of partners — all under the same governance, identity, and audit controls. The pilots are already proving it works — delivering measurable gains today, with six financial institutions co-developing the next wave with us.
[...]
Developed using AI-first engineering principles, agentOS combines enterprise-grade reliability with governance by design, including identity-bound execution, policy enforcement, observability and traceability. The platform allows financial institutions to deploy AI agents confidently across service, fraud, payments, compliance and growth workflows.
[...]
agentOS will initially feature four Fiserv agents: Commercial Loan Onboarding, Daily Operational Analysis and Reporting, Agentic Deposit Intelligence, and Agentic AML Triage Analysis. Fiserv will expand its portfolio of first-party agents in the future, with a focus on other time-consuming workflows—including fraud, servicing, compliance, risk management, deposit operations, and growth.
[...]
## Strategic Collaborators: OpenAI and Amazon Web Services
[...]
Fiserv is developing select first-party agents with OpenAI and partnering strategically to bring frontier reasoning into the workflows that move money across core, payments, issuer processing, and servicing.
[...]
“agentOS represents an important step toward helping financial institutions deploy AI agents in a secure, governed, and scalable way,” said Ashley Kramer, VP, Enterprise at OpenAI. “We’re glad to be working with Fiserv on this — bringing frontier AI into the banking workflows that matter most across servicing, compliance, fraud, payments, and operations.”
[...]
agentOS leverages Amazon Bedrock to provide secure access to leading AI models, giving financial institutions flexibility as the technology evolves. Fiserv will run agentOS on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, AWS‘s agentic platform for building, connecting, and optimizing agents securely at scale.
[...]
“Few industries set a higher bar for security, resilience, and accountability than banking. We’re proud that agentOS leverages Amazon Bedrock and AgentCore — bringing enterprise-grade AI infrastructure, multi-model flexibility, and the global scale of AWS to every institution Fiserv serves. We look forward to seeing what Fiserv, its clients, and its ecosystem of partners will build on this foundation,” said Scott Mullins, Managing Director, Worldwide Financial Services at AWS.
[...]
First Interstate Bank and Boulder Dam Credit Union are running pilots on agentOS today, with measurable results across commercial loan onboarding and operational reporting. Salem Five, City National Bank, Bank OZK, and SouthState are co-developing the next wave of agents with Fiserv, with deployments beginning this summer. Close collaboration with these institutions has been central to building an operating system that works for banks.
[...]
With agentOS, Fiserv brings the scale, expertise, and trust financial institutions require to deploy agentic AI within a governed, audit-ready architecture and convert it into durable operational progress.

Policy Management: Reviewing and updating security policies example, updating the Remote Work Policy to include guidelines for using generative AI. What would be great to have: Technical Foundation: General understanding of Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC) Android Development: Foundational experience in Android development (Android Studio, SDK components, and mobile UI basics). Technical Skills: Frameworks: Familiarity with PCI, NIST CSF, or SOC 2. Cloud Security: Basic understanding of GCP Cloud security. Data Privacy: General knowledge of GDPR or CCPA. AI Governance: Understanding the risks of LLMs (data leakage, prompt injection). Soft Skills: Attention to Detail: You are the "quality control" for security. Communication: Can you explain a technical risk to engineering or legal teams. Project Management: GRC involves tracking multiple small tasks simultaneously. What you will need to have: Recent graduate of a bachelor's degree completed in December 2024 or after in Computer Science, Data Science, Management Information Systems, Cybersecurity, Technical Project Management, or other Technology Degrees 3.0+ GPA 0-2 years of professional work experience Important info about this role: This role is on-site Monday through Friday. Fiserv considers in-person collaboration to be an essential part of this role as in-person office experiences help you with your overall onboarding experience and leads to stronger productivity. You must currently possess valid and unrestricted U.S. work authorization to be considered for this role. Individuals with temporary visas including, but not limited to, F-1 (OPT, CPT, STEM), H-1B, H-2, or TN, or any candidate requiring sponsorship, now or in the future, will not be considered for this role. This is a full-time, direct-hire position, and no contract options for unsolicited agency submissions will be considered. All offers of employment are contingent on standard background checks. Fiserv and certain of its affiliated companies are federal, state, and/or local government contractors. Should this position support a Federal Government contract, now or in the future, the successful candidate will be subject to a background check conducted by the U.S. Government to determine eligibility and suitability for federal contract employment for public trust or sensitive positions. Positions that support state and/or local contracts also may require additional background checks to determine eligibility and suitability. Application Ends: 3/20/26 Salary Range $70,800.00 - $106,200.00 These pay ranges apply to employees in Sunnyvale, California. Pay ranges for employees in other states may differ. It is unlawful to discriminate against a prospective employee due to the individual's status as a veteran. This position is not eligible for an annual incentive opportunity and is also not eligible to earn commissions. Thank you for

results.title: Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II | Wfhverse
highlights:
Generative Data Scientist II

Remote, USA

Posted May 31, 2026

Full-time

 Description Position Summary Details We’re hiring a Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II to design and deploy AI systems end-to-end: from data preparation and evaluation to model fine-tuning, inference, and agentic workflows. You’ll partner closely with product and engineering to ship reliable, cost-effective, and scalable LLM-powered solutions on AWS. Requirements Essential Duties & Responsibilities The essential functions include, but are not limited to the following: End-to-end GenAI solutions: Scope problems, choose the right approach (prompt engineering, fine-tuning, agents), implement, evaluate, and deploy. Data & SQL: Write efficient SQL for analytics and data preparation; manage schemas and pipelines for model training and inference. Model training & fine-tuning: Run supervised fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA), optimize prompts, and manage experiment tracking and evaluation. Agentic systems: Build agent workflows with tool use, memory, and safety/guardrails. Inference & deployment: Package services with Docker, optimize latency/cost (batching, caching, quantization), and deploy on AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda with GPU acceleration). MLOps & Observability: Set up CI/CD for models/prompts, maintain offline/online evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and rollback strategies. Security & compliance: Implement data governance, PHI/PII protections, and guardrails against prompt injection and unsafe outputs. Cross-functional work: Collaborate with product managers and engineers to align GenAI capabilities with product goals; document clearly and communicate trade-offs. Production readiness: Lead conversations around scaling, monitoring, and maintaining GenAI systems in real-world environments. Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities) Education: Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience required; Master’s degree preferred. Experience: 5+ years of Software/ML engineering experience, including 2+ years building and deploying GenAI/LLM systems. MS/PHD in Computer Science or equivalent experience. Strong SQL and Python skills with solid software engineering fundamentals. Experience with agent frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) and building tool-driven agents. Hands-on with deep learning (PyTorch or TensorFlow) and LLM fine-tuning(SFT/PEFT like LoRA/QLoRA). Production experience with Docker and deploying on AWS (ECS, EKS,SageMaker, Lambda, or GPU services). Experience creating Data and Model pipelines for model training anddeployment at scale. Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks (LLM-as-judge,metrics), and offline test harnesses. Understanding of security & compliance for sensitive data (e.g., PHI/PII) and safe deployment of AI systems. Excellent problem-solving, communication, and documentation skills. Inference optimization: quantization (bitsandbytes, GPTQ/AWQ), batching, caching, or vLLM. Healthcare experience: familiarity with HIPAA, medical data handling, or working in health tech. Experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B), CI/CD for ML, and monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana). Familiarity with major LLM APIs and OSS models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama, Mistral). Other: Tech Stack Languages: Python, SQL DL/LLM: PyTorch, Tensorflow, Hugging Face, PEFT/TRL, vLLM Data: Snowflake, Postgres Cloud: AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda) MLOps: Docker, CI/CD, MLflow or W&B Supervisory Responsibilities This role does not have any direct reports and is a single contributor role. Working Environment and Travel Requirements Work is typically in a normal office administrative environment involving minimal exposure to physical risks. Position requires little to moderate physical activity. Mostly sedentary work exerting up to 10 pounds of force occasionally or a negligible amount of force to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects. Work involves sitting most of the time, but may involve walking or standing for brief

results.title: Senior Generative AI Data Engineer - Financial Services - London - Jobs4LGBTQ+
highlights:
Senior Generative AI Data Engineer - Financial Services - London - Jobs4LGBTQ+
[...]
Senior Generative AI Data Engineer – Financial Services – London
[...]
Professional Communities: Data & AI
[...]
We are seeking a Senior Generative AI Data Engineer with strong experience in financial services (banking, capital markets, insurance, or fintech) to design, build, and scale GenAI-powered data platforms.
[...]
This role combines modern data engineering, LLM/AI integration, and financial domain expertise to deliver enterprise-grade solutions across use cases such as:
[...]
- Risk analytics & fraud detection
- Regulatory compliance automation
- Document intelligence (KYC, contracts, trade docs)
- Customer insights & conversational AI
[...]
You will work on cutting-edge technologies including LLMs, vector databases, RAG architectures, and cloud-native data platforms, helping organizations unlock value from structured and unstructured data.
[...]
## Your Role:
[...]
- Design and build end-to-end GenAI pipelines using LLMs (OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Anthropic)
- Develop RAG architectures with vector databases (Pinecone, FAISS, Azure AI Search)
- Optimize LLM outputs using prompt engineering, embeddings, and evaluation techniques
- Build AI solutions for document processing, compliance, and banking chatbots
- Develop scalable data pipelines using Spark, Databricks, Snowflake, and cloud platforms
- Implement batch and real-time data processing with structured and unstructured data
- Deploy solutions on cloud with CI/CD, MLOps tools, and ensure production reliability
- Collaborate with business teams, ensure compliance, and mentor teams on best practices
[...]
## Your Skills:
[...]
- 7+ years in data engineering or AI engineering with strong Python and SQL expertise
- Experience with LLM frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex) and vector databases
- Hands-on with distributed computing (Spark, Databricks) and microservices architecture
- Cloud experience with Azure (preferred), AWS, or GCP and lakehouse platforms
- Familiarity with DevOps tools (Git, CI/CD, Terraform) and Docker/Kubernetes
- Financial domain expertise in banking, capital markets, or fintech environments
- Knowledge of risk, compliance, KYC/AML, fraud systems, and regulatory workflows
- Strong problem-solving, communication, and stakeholder management skills in agile setups
[...]
Capgemini is an AI-powered global business and technology transformation partner, delivering tangible business value. We imagine the future of organisations and make it real with AI, technology and people. With our strong heritage of nearly 60 years, we are a responsible and diverse group of 420,000 team members in more than 50 countries. We deliver end-to-end services and solutions with our deep industry expertise and strong partner ecosystem, leveraging our capabilities across strategy, technology, design, engineering and business operations. The Group reported 2024 global revenues of €22.1 billion. Make it real | www.capgemini.com

Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
Generative artificial intelligence is transforming how we build products, serve clients, and operate at scale. In the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help turn advanced models into dependable, secure, and high-performing production services. You will work with partners across machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering to deliver solutions with clear return on investment. If you enjoy hands-on engineering, real-world constraints, and high-impact delivery, this role is for you. As a Senior Associate, Generative AI Engineer in the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help design, build, and support production generative artificial intelligence products and reusable backend application programming interfaces used across the firm. You will combine large enterprise datasets with large language and multimodal models to deliver scalable, measurable solutions. You will collaborate closely with machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering partners to ensure reliability, performance, and strong operational controls. You will contribute to technical design decisions, delivery planning, and continuous improvement of our platforms and products.Job responsibilities
